stimulus: Acclaimed nature documentarian Aroha Tane regards the field notebooks she keeps while observing wildlife in remote habitats to be a rewarding pursuit in their own right. ______blank Tane confesses that she finds the note-taking phase of her fieldwork just as captivating and absorbing as filming the animals themselves.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "Indeed" logically signals that the information in this sentence—that Tane finds the note-taking phase of her fieldwork to be just as captivating as filming animals—emphasizes and elaborates on the previous sentence's point that Tane regards her field notebooks as a rewarding pursuit.

Choice B is incorrect because "likewise" illogically signals that this sentence merely adds a second, similar point, when in fact it reinforces and strengthens the same point. Choice C is incorrect because "however" illogically signals that the information contrasts with the previous sentence, when it actually confirms it. Choice D is incorrect because "besides" illogically signals that the sentence provides a separate point in addition to the previous one, when in fact it intensifies the same claim.
